organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description:
Every company has a mission. What's ours? To empower every person and every organization to achieve more. We believe technology can and should be a force for good and that meaningful innovation contributes to a brighter world in the future and today. Our culture doesn’t just encourage curiosity; it embraces it. Each day we make progress together by showing up as our authentic selves. We show up with a learn-it-all mentality. We show up cheering on others, knowing their success doesn't diminish our own. We show up every day open to learning our own biases, changing our behavior, and inviting in differences. Because impact matters. 

Microsoft operates in 190 countries and is made up of approximately 228,000 passionate employees worldwide.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Founded in1967, ELTA Systems Ltd., a group and subsidiary of Israel Aerospace Industries, is one of Israel’s leading innovative defense companies and a global leader in the field of Intelligence, Surveillance, Mission Aircraft, Target Acquisition and Reconnaissance (ISTAR), Early Warning and Control, Homeland Security (HLS), Self-Protection and Self-Defense, Fire Control applications and Cyber Defense and Intelligence application.

ELTA offers comprehensive solutions, primarily based on in-house developed advanced electromagnetic sensors including Radar, Electronic  Warfare, and Communication Systems. As the Original Equipment Manufacturer of these sensors, ELTA’s integrated products and systems are tailored to the specific requirements of end-users, thereby creating a competitive advantage over leading international defense firms. Based on its advanced defense technologies, ELTA is also active in paramilitary and law enforcement markets. ELTA has a variety of unique technological excellence centers, state of the art facilities, and national infrastructures.

ELTA operates a worldwide marketing network, customer service and after-sales support activities.

At ELTA Systems Ltd we turn imagination into innovation, delivering the technology of the future - now.
We’re proud to design, develop and manufacture advanced solutions that give the edge to defense and security forces around the world.
companyResolutionResult.name: ELTA Systems Ltd

companyResolutionResult.description: Claroty has redefined cyber-physical systems (CPS) protection with an unrivaled industry-centric platform built to secure mission-critical infrastructure. The Claroty Platform provides the deepest asset visibility and the broadest, built-for-CPS solution set in the market comprising exposure management, network protection, secure access, and threat detection – whether in the cloud with Claroty xDome or on-premise with Claroty Continuous Threat Detection (CTD). Backed by award-winning threat research and a breadth of technology alliances, The Claroty Platform enables organizations to effectively reduce CPS risk, with the fastest time-to-value and lower total cost of ownership. Claroty is deployed by hundreds of organizations at thousands of sites globally. The company is headquartered in New York City and has a presence in Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America. To learn more, visit claroty.com.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

currentPositions.description:
As a software and infrastructure engineer in an elite technological unit, I led and supported a team of nine developers while driving critical DevOps and backend development initiatives. My work focused on building robust CI/CD pipelines with GitLab, JFrog, and Kaniko, and developing generic, scalable systems using Helm charts to improve deployment stability and speed.

Key contributions include:

Developed secure Python-based FastAPI servers with fine-grained permission control.

Engineered efficient pipelines that reduced buggy releases and enhanced delivery velocity.

Managed high-scale data pipelines, implementing optimized data flattening and retrieval.

Integrated and customized open-source tooling to streamline internal development processes.

Maintained system stability and code quality under mission-critical conditions.

I followed coding conventions, contributed to code reviews, and actively participated in an Agile development environment using Git, GitHub, and Jira.
I took full ownership of features from design to production with strong attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high-quality solutions.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Silverfort is on a mission to bring identity security everywhere, and allow organizations to operate without fear or disruptions. Fueled by a belief that enterprises and their identity teams deserve better, we found a way to break down the silos of identity security—eliminating the critical gaps and blind spots left behind by a patchwork of point solutions.

After years of research, we found a way to break free from these limitations. Silverfort created the only end-to-end identity security platform that secures all identities—humans and machines, on-prem and in the cloud. Our patented Runtime Access Protection (RAP) technology seamlessly integrates with the entire IAM infrastructure and secures it from within with unmatched visibility, analysis, and inline enforcement of security controls. This innovative approach brings protection to all resources in all environments, including ones that were previously impossible to secure, such as legacy systems, service accounts, command-line tools and IT/OT infrastructure. Finally, enterprises can stay ahead of the evolving identity threats, minimize their attack surface, stop breaches before they can spread, close compliance gaps, and break free from countless limitations and endless efforts.

Silverfort is the identity security platform the industry deserves, earning us the trust of more than 1,000 leading organizations, including several Fortune 50 companies. 

This is identity security done right.

Join Silverfort, and be part of a team that’s pushing the boundaries of identity security—no compromises, no blind spots, no limits.
companyResolutionResult.name: Silverfort

The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

currentPositions.description:
At one of the world’s leading mobile gaming companies, I manage a portfolio of high-value VIP players, applying analytical insights to maximize retention, LTV, and engagement.

*Manage a global portfolio of ~400 VIP clients contributing ~$185K/month in revenue.
*Use BI dashboards, Excel, and Google Sheets to track client behavior, purchase patterns, and campaign performance.
*Build short- and long-term engagement strategies based on player segmentation and marketing objectives.
*Collaborate with Product, Analytics, and Marketing to refine offers and develop high-conversion promotions.
*Run lifecycle campaigns focused on increasing ARPU, reducing churn, and boosting cross-game engagement.
*Create interactive social campaigns and events that enhance brand loyalty and player satisfaction.
*Provide feedback loops from clients to inform roadmap decisions, feature prioritization, and bug fixes.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

מטרת אוניברסיטת רייכמן (לשעבר הבינתחומי) היא להכשיר את מנהיגות העתיד של מדינת ישראל, באמצעות השכלה בינתחומית, המשלבת ידע תיאורטי מעמיק עם הכשרה מעשית, ובהנחיית סגל מרצים וחוקרים מהאוניברסיטאות המובילות בעולם ובישראל. גישתנו היא הקפדה על מצוינות במחקר ובהוראה, לצד דגש על אחריות חברתית ומעורבות בקהילה. מאז הקמתו, הצליח הבינתחומי הרצליה להיות חלוץ בתחומים רבים ולהוביל מהפכה במערכת ההשכלה הגבוהה בישראל, שאת עקבותיה ניתן לראות כיום במוסדות אקדמיים אחרים.

עקבו אחרינו בכל הרשתות החברתיות >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Eastern Mediterranean International School (EMIS) is a unique international boarding high school with a mission of making education a force for peace and sustainability in the Middle East.

Located in Hakfar Hayarok, a youth village and educational campus in the center of Israel, EMIS fosters intercultural understanding and promotes personal and social leadership. Through a shared educational and living experience, EMIS students gain the values of respect, diversity, and global citizenship. 

Our students complete the prestigious two-year International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Program. Students can choose to spend 3 years at EMIS by doing a Pre-IB year in 10th grade. All students also participate in our broad range of rich and unique extracurricular and community programs!

currentPositions.description:
As a Social Guide for students at the Eastern Mediterranean International School (EMIS), I am entrusted with the wellbeing and development of a diverse, international student group of over 30+ students. My role encompasses a wide range of responsibilities focused on ensuring a supportive and enriching boarding school experience. I oversee many aspects, from the logistics of boarding life, room assignments and daily routines to fostering a well rounded educational process personalized to the needs of each one of my students.

Collaboration is key in my role, as I work closely with other boarding staff, academic staff and the school counselor to address the wide range of student needs. I foster a positive group dynamic through various social activities and mentorship, guiding students in forming meaningful connections and promoting inclusivity. My efforts help cultivate an environment where students can thrive both personally and academically.

currentPositions.description:
Side by side with my role as a social guide at EMIS, as Digital Transformation and Automation Manager, I lead initiatives to integrate cutting‐edge digital solutions to improve daily operations, drawing on my background from the military, newly acquired skills and my passion for innovation. 

My key responsibilities include:
- Driving Digital Innovation: Designing, developing, and maintaining an in-house automation application that streamlines workflows and enhances team productivity.

- Strategic Technology Integration: Collaborating with EMIS staff members in all areas to identify inefficiencies and implement agile, technology-driven improvements.

- Consulting & Advisory: Acting as a digital consultant to guide EMIS through its transformation journey—ensuring the effective integration of automation tools and digital systems.
        
- Change Management: Leveraging leadership and project management skills honed in military and educational settings to manage change and drive continuous improvement of existing and new digital solutions.   

- Staying Ahead of Trends: Monitoring emerging technologies and best practices to deliver innovative solutions that support our educational mission.
